channel.title: Comments for page "A Wandsman in the Court of the Hanged King"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "A Wandsman in the Court of the Hanged King"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If anyone wants more in this vein, try the purple-prose masterpiece The Dream-Quest of Unknown Kadath by H. P. Lovecraft.

+1 for flashbacks to that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've got to say, I've been having Algadda on the mind and this tale really hits the spot. Take yet another +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought this was thoroughly wonderful. My only gripe is that it's an SCP tale rather than being a Wanderer's Library tale. I hate that The Library is dying, and I feel that these sorts of otherworldly tales belong there even if they are related to scips.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I haven't the faintest idea what in the hell I just read but I know I enjoyed it immensely. I've been enraptured by Alagadda since my first reading of SCP-701, and I'm thrilled that there's more to discover. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ooo, I love this so much! upvote from me. I could literally hear the voice of the character, which is not an easy feat, my man. Well done!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder if he ever came across J.C. Randall in his travels

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think you actually succeded perfectly in capturing the "feeling" of Alagadda in the actual SCP. I however really like the Wandsman as a character, and I definitely want to see more of him.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I would like to see The Wandsman become a recurring character, occasionally alluded to in extra-dimensional SCPs and with a few more tales detailing there wanderings.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Those illustrations are awesome…

/me lures Sunny into his talebox

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It might be the "zr" sound, or it could be that 'does' is just slightly hard. It parses fine where I sit, though.

Also, I forgot to say it earlier, but shame organs. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now that I am completely unsure of my ability to do this:

"atop" seems to be a trochaic foot. The other three seem to be iambic. Changing "atop" to "on" and adding a one unstressed syllable adjective to throne, should fix it. I think, but I am now unsure if I wasn't taught wrong as a joke. :(

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: How so? Please enlighten me, (preferably with diacritical notation).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "Our Lord does writhe atop his throne" is still bothering me - did even before posting. But struggled (and failed) to get it right.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm a poetry Nazi, it's my Thing. :B Like I said, having the last couplet not follow the rest distinctly works, given the context.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll make those changes. I've never been able to actually follow stress/unstress stuff;/ Like, in my mind, I struggle to make out the difference. The last part is not meant to follow the pattern of the rest.

Edit: Changed based on what you said, but would like to fix up some other things. The first line of the final stanza no longer feels quite right with the change to the second line. Ugh, I really wish I could *feel* those sounds better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dunno, but what you've written definitely is not. :/

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Supposed to be iambic tetrameter?

"Thus before his throne, we atone"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can never keep track of shit, so aside from the Hanged King, this was all new to me. I have to hand it to you, I'm gonna point people to this to show them how to get random, otherworldly stuff to sound right.

And now, if I may, a look at your poetry...
Cuz it done druv me up tha wizzall

Stranger that which is found within,

I parsed this a number of times as "A stranger that", but I see what you're getting at. To mend the meter as well as be clearer, try "Stranger yet is found within", or maybe "still" instead of "yet".

Here the mad dance without control,

"The mad dance here without control," ('The' is a soft syllable and doesn't mess up the meter quite as much at the beginning.)

And before his glory do we atone,

This needs help. "Before his glory, we atone" is the best I can do.

With this, our blood, it is the Hanged King's,
So shall we suffocate upon his puppet-strings.

These two are a mess, and I could maybe suggest ways to bring them into order. On the other hand, ending with a couplet that's completely nuts, vis-a-vis metrics, actually drives home the chaos and otherworldliness, so I think you could let it stand. However, to make it work, the rest needs to be flawless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes - it is a difficult history to explore though - it is one of those things whose truth might undermine the whole concept (as much as I love to explore history).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That was my intention - there was a *feeling* of Alagadda that just couldn't translate in a SCP format. And ever since writing 2264, I've wanted to explore that - the Wandsman, through his dramatic writings and special senses, seemed like the perfect vessel to do so, especially when dealing with such a dreamlike place.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fantastic. The standard interactions between the king and the ambassador are getting a bit stale, however. If we're going to hear more about Alagadda, I'd like to see more about their past. Maybe explore a bit of how they got to where they are… or the nature of the beauty of the one version of Alagadda compared to the fallen part. Either way, +1 for now because that artwork is damn near magical.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted without hesitation. :) I hope to see more tales involving the Wandsman, he's a fascinating character.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My thoughts in particular. My upvote stems less from having seriously new content as from the enjoyment I got out of the act of reading the prose. I like the aesthetic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Metaphysician, why you so epic?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Pretty much the same as 2264 in terms of content, nothing new learnt except for a few bits here and there. But, it's more Alagadda so it earns a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Edit: ALMOST FORGOT! Special thanks to SunnyParallax for their wonderful artwork!

Right before posting, I cut something out of the very end.

"Closure: Nine of Ten. Would visit again."
